applicant_information.title: Owner & Lead Guide

activity_or_event_details.describe_the_activity_or_event_and_the_l:
We are requesting a commercial use permit for guided hiking and beginner-level mountain biking tours within DuPont State Recreational Forest, primarily focused around the High Falls, Hooker Falls, and Lake Imaging trail systems. Our business, Blue Ridge Adventure Outfitters, LLC, is a small, locally owned outdoor guiding company based in Brevard, NC that specializes in low-impact, educational outings for families, small corporate retreat groups, and visitors who are new to the area.

TYPE OF ACTIVITY:
We plan to offer half-day and full-day guided outings that combine interpretive hiking with optional beginner-friendly mountain biking. The emphasis is on safety, Leave No Trace principles, and environmental education. Typical outings include:

1) WATERFALL HIKING TOUR (ON FOOT):
- Primary Locations: High Falls, Triple Falls (viewed from designated overlooks and trail corridors), and Hooker Falls.
- Description: Groups meet at the High Falls access area parking lot, receive a safety briefing, and then hike to the High Falls and Triple Falls overlooks using existing, designated trails. For groups including young children or less mobile participants, we shorten the route and focus more time at the High Falls and Hooker Falls viewing areas. We do not allow participants to leave designated trails or climb on wet rocks near the falls. Throughout the hike, guides provide information on local geology, forest ecology, and the history of DuPont State Recreational Forest, including its transition from private industrial land to a public recreation and conservation resource.

2) BEGINNER MOUNTAIN BIKE TOUR (ON BIKES):
- Primary Locations: Lake Imaging access area and associated beginner-friendly trails (e.g., Lake Imaging Road, locally recognized easy loops and connectors appropriate for novice riders).
- Description: Participants meet at the Lake Imaging access area. We conduct a pre-ride safety talk covering helmet use, braking techniques, trail etiquette, and yield rules (especially with respect to hikers and equestrians). We then ride short, low-elevation-gain loops on wide, established trails suitable for beginners. Our guides constantly monitor rider comfort and adjust the route accordingly. We avoid any advanced or technical singletrack. The focus is on building confidence and teaching responsible trail use rather than on speed or distance.

GROUP SIZE & STRUCTURE:
Our typical group size is 6–10 participants per guide, with a maximum of 12 participants per guide on foot and 8 participants per guide on bikes. For larger bookings (for example, corporate retreats or extended families), we add additional guides to maintain safe ratios. Groups stay together; we do not allow participants to explore on their own outside of the structured outing.

SAFETY & RISK MANAGEMENT:
All guides maintain current Wilderness First Aid or higher certifications and carry first-aid kits, extra water, and emergency contact information. We conduct a verbal health and fitness check with participants at the trailhead and reserve the right to modify the route or turn back if conditions (weather, trail crowding, or participant fatigue) warrant it. We monitor weather forecasts closely and will cancel or reschedule trips in the event of thunderstorms, ice, or other hazardous conditions.

ENVIRONMENTAL & COMMUNITY CONSIDERATIONS:
We adhere strictly to Leave No Trace principles. We pack out all trash, do not allow participants to pick plants or disturb wildlife, and educate visitors about staying on designated trails to protect sensitive habitats and minimize erosion. We also emphasize respectful behavior toward other forest users, including yielding appropriately and keeping noise levels low at scenic viewpoints and waterfall overlooks.

SCHEDULE & FREQUENCY:
During the requested permit period, we anticipate offering up to 3 guided outings per week during peak season (spring through early fall), with fewer outings during winter months depending on conditions. We typically schedule morning starts to avoid crowding and afternoon thunderstorms, but will occasionally host an afternoon or early evening outing for sunset views when appropriate and safe.

SUMMARY OF LOCATIONS TO BE PERMITTED:
- High Falls access area: Used as the primary trailhead for waterfall hiking tours to High Falls and Triple Falls overlooks.
- Hooker Falls access area: Used for shorter, family-friendly waterfall hikes and for accessing Hooker Falls viewing areas.
- Lake Imaging access area: Used as the staging area and primary trail network for beginner mountain bike tours on wide, established trails.

We are not requesting permission for any exclusive use of trails or facilities and fully understand that all areas remain open to the public at all times. Our goal is to complement existing recreational use by providing structured, educational experiences that help visitors enjoy DuPont State Recreational Forest safely and responsibly.

insurance.liability_insurance_company_name: Carolina Outdoor & Recreation Insurance Group, Inc.
